observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Instruct the MAO to ensure that the LRN Ledger Cards contain complete and accurate information;
Task the MGSO with completing the LRN Property Cards, ensuring all essential details are included; and
Require the IC to thoroughly prepare the RPCLRN, addressing any gaps in information to facilitate proper verification.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to rigorously monitor and enforce compliance with remittance regulations and implement the following directives for revenue collectors:
Ensure prompt remittance of collections in adherence to the prescribed remittance schedule; and
Mandate the preparation of the RCD upon each collection remittance, highlighting the actual date of remittance to the MTO.
Further, we recommended and Management agreed to continue the adherence to this good practice and strong coordination between these offices.

We commended the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer for their teamwork and dedication in preparing the CY 2024 BRS for all 18 bank accounts of the MGI. Their efforts ensured accurate financial records, compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations, and strengthened trust in the MGI’s financial management.
To sustain this success, we recommended and Management agreed to continue regular reconciliation schedules, improving communication, and conducting periodic training to enhance their skills and uphold accuracy and transparency in financial processes.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to properly identify and distinguish operational expenses for the MGI and NGAs during budget preparation, ensuring separate budgeting and clear identification of NGA-related expenses;
Require the Municipal Accountant to properly record all operational expenses paid by the MGI on behalf of NGAs under the Subsidy to NGAs account for accurate financial classification and reporting; and
Ensure coordination among the MBO, the Municipal Accountant, and concerned department heads to maintain transparency, monitoring, and accountability over funds extended to NGAs.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MA to:
Determine the validity of the unpaid AP amounting to ₱33,796.10 which have been outstanding for more than two years and if found to have no legitimate claim against the Municipality, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus; and
Ensure that all JEVs are supported by complete documentary evidence, i.e., DRs and AIRs, demonstrating that the goods or services were delivered to, or received by, the Municipality, that would warrant the recording of the AP.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Thoroughly evaluate the proposed priority development PPAs under the 20% DF to ensure their readiness for implementation or procurement, thereby preventing delays or non-implementation;
Ensure the timely execution of all PPAs to maximize the efficient use of public funds and delivery of the intended benefits to the community; and
Conduct an extensive review of the unimplemented PPAs to determine whether these projects are still feasibly implementable and reprogram those that are considered non-priority PPAs.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with Items 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in order to come up with a complete and comprehensive plan that will serve as basis in the implementation of the MGI’ DRRM PPAs.
Likewise, we reiterated our previous recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Enjoin the MDRRMO to exert more effort in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of DRRM towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and communities;
Monitor closely the utilization and implementation of all PPAs under the said fund to facilitate the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization; and
Require the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis to the AT within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal HRM Officer (MHRMO) to:
Prioritize the formulation of a “Qualification Standards and Salary Rates for JO Positions” to come up with a HRP to be submitted to the SB for subsequent enactment of a resolution adopting the same; and
Issue a uniform policy guideline on the hiring of JO personnel to properly determine the necessity of hiring JOs and to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto.
Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to observe the provisions of Section 2, Rule XI of CSC MC No. 40, series of 1998 and Section 211 (b) of the GAAM, Volume I, in resorting to hiring of casual or emergency staff to avoid hiring a significant number of JO personnel.
Further, we recommended and the Committee on Rules, Privileges and Amendments of the SB agreed to take appropriate action to address all matters pertaining to the organization and management; personnel administration, position classification and pay plan, staffing patterns; and creation of positions and policy formulation, in connection with the observations noted in audit.

We recommended and the IMSB agreed to consider the following measures to strengthen its SEF planning and budgeting processes:
Strictly comply with the budget calendar of activities as outlined in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 to ensure timely and efficient utilization of the SEF resources, aligning with the fiscal calendar and guidelines set forth for the effective implementation of education PPAs;
Consider actual cash availability in preparing supplemental budgets by including both the unexpended balances from previous years and the excess of actual income over projections to guarantee realistic financial planning and optimal allocation of resources to priority education PPAs; and
Use a needs-based and evidence-supported budgeting approach to ensure that resources are allocated to school priorities and initiatives outlined in the DepEd-approved SIPs and DEDP, promoting targeted and impactful interventions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Create a GAD M&E Team to facilitate the evaluation of the outcome of the Municaplity’s GAD policies, programs and projects and to determine areas for improvement for mainstreaming activities; and
Direct the GFP to verify the reported Accomplishment Reports submitted by each implementing office by reconciling the data with the records from the MBO, and MA to ensure consistency thereby increasing the reliability of GAD reporting.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the GFPS to undertake the following measures:
Set-up the GAD Database from including sex-disaggregated data to facilitate gender analysis thereby supporting priority gender issues affecting the Municipality;
Capacitate GFPS members on the proper use and application of GAD tools such as the HGDG, PEMMI, and FEMMI, to ensure that PPAs are responsive and appropriately attributed; and
Integrate the results of HGDG assessments into the GPB and GAD AR to enhance transparency, monitoring, and evaluation of GAD-related interventions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) pursue authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to donate/transfer the PPEs currently utilized by the other government agencies. Subsequently, immediate action should be taken to facilitate the transfer of ownership.  Following this, it is imperative to submit the necessary documents to the Municipal Accountant’s Office (MAO) to remove these properties from the books of accounts, in accordance with the guidelines set forth in Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that Management minimize the incurrence of excessive and unnecessary catering services/meals/snacks to the extent allowed by law. 

If the same cannot be avoided, we recommended that Management ensure that the meals, snacks and catering services for special occasions, conferences and seminars are duly supported with guests list or prospective attendees in order to arrive at the most proximate number of attendees. 

Likewise, we recommend that other cost of meals and snacks be charged to the monthly representation allowance of department heads. 

Additionally, to ensure transparency in procurement:

Specify the Approved Budget for the Contract in the Request for Quotation (RFQ); and

Plan events in advance and consolidate all catering service expenses for the MGI to conduct procurement through public bidding.

We recommended that Management:

Observe proper charging of expenditures from the 20% DF to avoid disallowance in audit and to ensure optimal attainment of desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the MGI;

Evaluate thoroughly the proposed priority development PPAs to be funded under the 20% DF as to their readiness for implementation or procurement to avoid delays or non-implementation thereof.

Ensure the timely implementation of all PPAs whether funded by current or continuing appropriations; and

Ensure the completeness and accuracy of the reports produced to provide relevant information to decision makers.

We recommended that Management:

Enjoin the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Officer to exert more effort in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of DRRM towards building a disaster-resilient Local Government Unit (LGU) and communities;

Monitor closely the implementation of all PPAs under the LDRRMF to facilitate the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization; and

Stop the practice of using the LDRRMF for expenditures not in accordance with National DRRM Council, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the LDRRMF is used strictly for DRRM activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Municipal DRRM Officer to include in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; and

Require the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant (MA) to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis to the AT within the prescribed period.

Likewise, we recommended that the MA maintain SLs for the unutilized DRRMF in accordance with Section 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to donate the various existing properties being used by the Barangays in their localities, and facilitate immediately the subsequent transfer thereof.

Thereafter, submit the relevant documents to the OMA to drop the subject properties from the books in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386.

We commended Management for its notable work accomplishments relative to the utilization of the 20% DF.  Accordingly, the AT recommended that Management continue its improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing PPAs to maximize the utilization of the said fund for the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

Likewise, we recommended that Management:

Direct the concerned departments to revisit the feasibility of implementing the PPAs that remained unexecuted as of December 31, 2022 for possible reprogramming/reallocation to other priority PPAs; and

Monitor closely the implementation of all PPAs under the 20% DF to ensure prompt completion and to facilitate the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund, if needed, to other prioritized PPAs to maximize the utilization thereof.

We commended Management for the substantial compliance with the salient provisions of COA Circular 2012-001.

We likewise recommended that Management require the Municipal DRRM Office (MDRRMO) to include in the preparation of the annual LDRRMFIP for the succeeding years the list of PPAs charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.

We recommended that Management require the Rural Health Unit (RHU) to:

Coordinate with the PGS to secure the documents that contain the costs of the items donated as needed for the recording thereof; and

Ensure to align the inventory procedures with the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Office and RHU to plan properly the utilization and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines, especially those nearly expiring to prevent wastage thereof.

We commended Management particularly the MTO for the efficient collection of RPT and SET as it was able to exceed the target collections set by the BLGF.

However, we recommended that Management:

Intensify the tax dissemination/campaign by sending MTO personnel to barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive to encourage more taxpayers to pay taxes on time; 

Continue the issuance of demand letters/notice of delinquencies to all delinquent taxpayers to settle immediately their unpaid taxes; and

Require the MTO to post in conspicuous places and publicly accessible places within the municipality the tax information and send out notices to taxpayers as part of the tax campaign.

We recommended that the Municipal Human Resource Management (MHRM) Officer:

Prepare a policy adopting a schedule of daily wage rates of JOs based on qualification as well as the nature of work/assignment to be performed; and

Issue written guidelines and procedures to evaluate the work performance of hired JO personnel.

We commended Management’s efforts in integrating GAD in its policy-making through the enactment of a GAD Code.

Nevertheless, we reiterated our recommendations that the GFPS:

Complete the gathering of sex-disaggregated data to establish a GAD database, placing priority on the data relevant to the GAD issues indicated in the GPB to ensure that the said issues represent actual gender gaps and/or differences among the constituents of the Municipality; and

Allot the necessary budget in setting-up and maintaining the GAD database.
We recommended that the LCE require the MEO and the OMA to reconcile periodically their reports on completed projects particularly those recorded under the CIP accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require the:

OMA and the MGSO/MT to prepare and submit the necessary reports and records pertaining to local road network in accordance with the prescribed formats as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008;

MA and the MGS Officer/MT to conduct regular reconciliation of LRN Ledger Cards (LRNLC) and the LRN Property Cards (LRNPC) at least on quarterly basis; and

ME to furnish the MA and the MGS Officer/MT with complete descriptions and cost segregations of various road components of completed road projects as basis for recording in their respective records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to consider hiring or designating a GSO supported with necessary staffs, to perform and strengthen property management and custodianship.
We also recommended and Management agreed that the designated GSO:
Issue demand letters signed by the LCE to the concerned accountable officers (AO) to produce the PPE items not found during the inventory count.  Moreover, require written justification from the concerned AOs if the same is unable to produce the said PPE items; and
Prepare the required PCs to facilitate reconciliation of reported PPE balances.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the MEO to request the concerned contractors to submit their claims and other documents needed for final payment of unpaid contracts.  Also, require the MEO and other concerned Offices to collate supporting documents and/or prepare payrolls to process the unpaid labor costs of projects implemented by administration;
Require the MEO and the MAO to reconcile periodically their reports on completed projects particularly those recorded under the CIP accounts; and
Require the MA to effect the necessary adjusting entries for the following: (1) to derecognize from the books the amount recorded for the Temporary Facilities for CY 2020 Jamboree; (2) to reclassify the CIP accounts for projects already completed to the appropriate PPE accounts; and (3) to recognize the corresponding accumulated depreciation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for the Municipal Mayor to donate the various PPE to recipient schools actually using these; and
Thereafter, furnish the MAO of the SB Resolution and Deed of Donation as basis for dropping from the books the subject properties in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the collectors, through the MT:
to remit promptly their collections in accordance with the prescribe period of remittance;
to prepare always the RCD every time they remit their collections to show the actual date of remittance to the MTO; and
to prepare individual cashbooks and to monitor properly their collections and accountable forms.
We also recommended and Management agreed to direct the MT to monitor and enforce strictly the daily remittance of collections by revenue collectors to avoid accumulation of cash and possible loss thereof through theft or misappropriation.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all of its officers and employees who are in control of the MGI’s funds and property are properly bonded.  Likewise, we recommended that Management ensure that the CA allowed to disbursing officers must be within their limit of maximum cash accountability as approved by the BTr to recover any losses from unwanted events.

We recommended and Management agreed that the MA prepare and maintain updated SLs in such detail as is necessary to meet the needs of the users/agency and at the same time be adequate to furnish the information needed by fiscal or control agencies of the government.  Also, we recommended that the AO certify the cashbooks at the end of each month and provide the dates of all CAs.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the MAO to:
Assess the collectability of the outstanding CAs and if warranted, recommend to the LCE to request from COA for write-off of accounts in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2016-005; and
Continue the good practice of monitoring the CAs granted to officials and employees to ensure timely liquidation thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Review and analyze the existing MOA between the LGU and the DA RFO No. 5 and address the ambiguous provisions;
Secure the revised MOA or supplemental MOA or related documents that can address or clarify the obligations of both parties; and
Thereafter, release immediately to the chosen farmers-organization the allotted amount after complying with the needed documentary requirements.
Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to revisit the MOA between the MGI and the IFRAC to clarify uncertain provisions in the MOA.  If necessary, revised the same to include only those provisions which are applicable and appropriate.

We recommended and Management agreed to avoid utilizing the LDRRMF for non-disaster risk related expenditures so as not to defeat the purpose of RA No 10121.  Likewise, we recommended that Management return the funds used for the procurement of ATVs to the LDRRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS observe strictly the schedule in the preparation and submission of the GPB and GAR to ensure timely submission of the said reports to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office for its review and endorsement.

We recommended and Management directed the GFPS to:
Complete the gathering of sex-disaggregated data to establish a GAD database, placing priority on data relevant to GAD issues indicated in the GPB to ensure that said issues represent actual gender gaps and/or differences among the constituents of the Municipality; and
Allot budget for the setting-up and maintenance of the GAD database.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the MHRMO to submit to the MAO the lacking HDMF numbers of the job order personnel with unremitted contributions.  Otherwise, require the job order employees to apply for Pag-IBIG MID and inform immediately the MAO of the MID for remittance purposes;; and
Comply continuously with existing laws, rules and regulations on deductions and remittance of HDMF contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply continuously with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deduction and remittance of PhilHealth contributions in accordance with Section 18 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 7875 as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606.
We recommended and Management agreed to follow up the response of the SP of PGS on the reply filed by MGI relative to the invalidated budget with end view of securing an operative budget.
Further, we recommended that Management hire or designate a GSO together with its staffs, if funds permit, in order to have a check and balance between the property and accounting records.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office:
Continue the good practice in monitoring the cash advances granted to the municipal officials and employees;
Issue demand letters compelling the officials or employees to immediately settle in full their outstanding cash advances, either by liquidation, by payment/refund or through salary deductions, whichever is applicable; and
Strictly comply with the requirements of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-001 and all other related rules and regulations pertaining to the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that Management ensure that the shares in RPT collections be released to the concerned barangays within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160 so that these funds could be promptly used in their operations and implementation of the projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Provide legal basis for the method of distribution of SET proceeds which pertains to CY 1991 and prior, otherwise, follow the provisions of the LGC of 1991;
Require the Municipal Treasurer/Municipal Accountant to confirm with the Provincial Government as to the balance of unremitted SET of the Municipality as of December 31, 2020; and
Remit or utilize the subject amount of the Due to LGUs account.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of RPT and SET due and collectible based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card and submit this to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year to set up the receivables; and
Municipal Accountant set up the receivable based on the list submitted by the MTO.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the discounts on RPT/SET in the year the taxes are applicable in accordance with Section 22 of NGAS Manual for LGUs. Likewise, we recommended to correct the errors committed in the recording of discount during the year hence to present only the actual discount availed by the taxpayers applicable for the year.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a detailed schedule and ageing of Guaranty Deposit Payable account under the GF, SEF and  TF;
Revert to the unappropriated surplus those identified as long outstanding payables wherein the obligation to pay had been prescribed as provided in the applicable law or wherein the guarantee deposit had been forfeited; and
Cause the release of the retention money to its suppliers/contractors in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Enjoin the LDRRMO to exert more effort in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk reduction and management towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and communities; and
Closely monitor the implementation of all PPAs under the LDRRMF to facilitate corrective action, if any, and the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization.

Instruct the MPDC, in consultation with the concerned departments to revisit the feasibility of the implementation of the PPAs which remain unimplemented as of December 31, 2020 for possible reprogramming/reallocation to other COVID-19 related PPAs , considering the restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to record the properties over which the Municipality has acquired absolute ownership.

We recommended that Management adhere to the documentary requirements pursuant to Section 4, paragraph 6 of PD No. 1445 and DSWD MC No. 2015-004. We recommended that Management strictly maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.

We recommended that:
The Local Finance Committee and the Budget Office as the lead office:
Conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets;
Review the present budgeting to set a clearer guide for the concerned revenue generating Departments towards attaining the income projections; and
Provide for the targets on income accounts that did not have projections which must be based on statistics, prior years’ experience and future expectation.
The concerned Municipal Officials conduct periodic review to ascertain whether collection targets are attained. The causes for the shortfall in collections must be analyzed and alternative measures must be looked into to avoid incurrence of misses in projections in the future; and
The Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording of income accounts balance in the FS and ensure that all transactions are recorded in conformity with the Revised Chart of Accounts promulgated by the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

with the requirements of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular Nos. 01 s. 1998, 01-A s. 2000 and 01-B s. 2001, as follows:

a. Identify the PPAs that reflect an equitable distribution of the mandated
priority expenses and refrain from using the SEF outside the scope for
which it was established to avoid disallowance in audit;

b. Maximize the implementation of the identified and prioritized PPAs to
ensure efficient utilization of the fund so that its objectives are fully
achieved; and

c. Strictly monitor the budget to anticipate the timely re-alignment of funds
for other allowable PPAs in lieu of those that cannot be implemented.

We recommended that Management encourage the LDRRMO to exert more
effort in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk
reduction and management towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and
communities.

Also, we recommended that Management closely monitor the
implementation of all PPAs under the LDRRMF to facilitate corrective action, if
any, and the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum
utilization.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require
the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the required
property records for proper identification and monitoring of all the PPE of the
Municipality.

We recommended that Management expedite the disposal of unserviceable
properties in accordance with the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No.

92-386.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management:

a. Expedite the completion/updating of the 10-year Municipal SWM Plan
for approval by the NSWMC to serve as guide for the efficient
management of solid waste generated within the Municipality’s
jurisdiction; and

b. Strictly comply with RA No. 9003 on the establishment of solid waste
facility.

We recommended that Management:

a. Exert conscientious efforts in incurring expenses that would not
arbitrarily compromise funds intended for other essential undertakings;
and

b. In planning for any activity, consider adopting cost-benefit analysis to
measure whether the expenses to be incurred will redound to the benefit
of most of the residents and determine events that will be most
advantageous to the government.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort in preparing and submitting on time
the subject accounts and financial reports including the supporting documents. We
suggested that such activities be properly monitored so that reports are submitted
promptly in accordance with the salient provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA
Circular No. 2009-006.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management strictly
comply with DBM and DILG JMC No. 2011-1, as follows:

a. Maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund and
implement the programs, projects and activities within the targeted
timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes
essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired
quality of life for all; and

Develop a concrete plan that will articulate the program, project and
activity, timeline, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones,
etc.
